evidence_quotes.quote: With the flick of a switch, the game we paid for is gone forever.

paragraphs.text: This is a fantastic proposal, but it overlooks one of the biggest scams in modern gaming. While we absolutely need to tackle predatory loot boxes, data selling, and subscription traps, we also need to talk about publishers having a 'kill switch' for games we've already bought. The problem is 'always-online' games. We pay full price for a title, and years later the publisher can decide it's not worth keeping the servers running. With the flick of a switch, the game we paid for is gone forever. Imagine buying a book, but the publisher could decide to remotely set it on fire on your bookshelf years later. That's what this is. It's an unfair practice that allows companies to take our money and then destroy the product we bought. The EU Fairness Act must also protect our digital purchases. When we buy a game, it should be ours to keep. The Stop Killing Games movement and EU petition are one of the best way to achieve this currently. I appreciate you taking this feedback into consideration!
